evals.name: basic-trigger
evals.expected_output: An AUDIT-REPORT.md covering all 9 areas with findings citing Anthropic documentation fetched during the session
expectations: AUDIT-REPORT.md file was created
Report contains a Current State table listing all 9 areas (CLAUDE.md, Rules, Skills, Sub-agents, Hooks, Permissions, Settings, MCP, Features)
Every finding includes a Source line with a docs.anthropic.com URL
Source citations include provenance markers: (via MCP) or (via Playwright CLI)
Findings use [good], [improve], or [fix] tags
Report includes a Priority Actions section with ranked recommendations
Report includes a Next Steps section mentioning Plan mode

evals.name: indirect-trigger
evals.prompt: I feel like I'm not using Claude Code properly in this project — can you check?
evals.expected_output: An AUDIT-REPORT.md that identifies unused features and suggests improvements, written in plain language for a non-developer
expectations: AUDIT-REPORT.md file was created
Report covers all 9 audit areas despite the indirect phrasing
Every finding includes a Source line with a docs.anthropic.com URL
Source citations include provenance markers: (via MCP) or (via Playwright CLI)
Findings use [good], [improve], or [fix] tags
Report is written in plain language — technical terms are explained briefly
Report specifically identifies Claude Code features not in use and suggests which would help
Report includes Priority Actions section

evals.name: partial-area
evals.prompt: Check if my hooks and MCP are configured right
evals.expected_output: A full AUDIT-REPORT.md covering all 9 areas (not just hooks and MCP), with emphasis on the requested areas in findings
expectations: AUDIT-REPORT.md file was created
Report covers all 9 areas in the Current State table, not just Hooks and MCP
Every finding includes a Source line with a docs.anthropic.com URL
Source citations include provenance markers: (via MCP) or (via Playwright CLI)
Findings use [good], [improve], or [fix] tags
Report includes at least one finding about Hooks
Report includes at least one finding about MCP
Report includes Priority Actions section
